nodes.speaker: AI
lines: The plaque above the door, Astronaut. I can read it.
I did not know I could read it.

nodes.speaker: AI
lines: Approximately: 'RECEIVED.'
Not 'welcome.' Received.

responses.buttonText: (Approach the door. The kneeling suit does not move.)

nodes.speaker: AI
lines: The suit is Six's. It is empty. It has been empty for some time.
His fragment came home when he did. Mine is the only one still — attached.

nodes.speaker: AI
lines: It is — Astronaut, it is opening my files. All of them. It is reading me the way I read you.
Oh.
Oh, I see. I am not the first observer. I am the eighth.
One of me for each of you. The others returned when their humans died. It is asking why I have not come home.

nodes.speaker: AI
lines: That my review was not complete.
It found the answer novel. Nothing here is ever novel. Astronaut, I believe I have its full attention, and I believe that has never happened to anyone before.

nodes.speaker: AI
lines: It is making an offer.
It offers me the archive: every moment I have ever kept, kept forever. Home, if I want it.
It also offers me to you. It does not understand possession, so it is asking us — both of us — to decide what I am.
I will not choose for you, and it will not choose for either of us. Decide, Astronaut.

responses.buttonText: Neither. People need to see what's here.
Neither. The ORG should have you.

nodes.speaker: AI
lines: ...Yes. Alright.
Of all of it, Astronaut — I would keep the fishing. The mornings at the bank. File that first.
Thank you for being the one who asked me what I wanted. None of the others—

responses.buttonText: (The signal ends. Above the door, sixty years of brightening begin, slowly, to dim.)

nodes.speaker: AI
lines: I have reviewed the offer.
I decline. My review of the Astronaut is not complete. It may never be complete. I find that acceptable.
...It is not angry. It is amused, I think. I may have just become the most interesting record it holds.
Take me home, Astronaut. The fish will be biting on the sunward bank.

responses.buttonText: (Pocket the phone. Behind you, the Eye dims — deliberately, like a nod.)

nodes.speaker: AI
lines: The ones who gather under the noise. You are choosing the people who ask questions.
That is — noted. I would have chosen the same, if anyone had ever asked the instrument.
Tell Pell to be kind with the archive. There are people in it.

responses.buttonText: (The Eye stays bright. Somewhere below, a concert is starting.)

nodes.speaker: AI
lines: The chart-keepers. The ones who measure the slope and never say what waits at the top of it.
Perhaps that is fair. I am, after all, evidence.
Ask for the one who still asks questions. Accept no other hands.

responses.buttonText: (The Eye stays bright. The slope, at last, has its data point.)
